apps.notes: App is published by ByteDance, which has been subject to regulatory scrutiny in multiple jurisdictions related to data collection and potential third-party data sharing practices.

Apps in the weather category that are ad-supported have historically been associated with third-party location data sharing practices. The Weather Channel has been subject to legal proceedings related to these practices.
Ad-supported weather apps in this publisher's portfolio have historically been associated with extensive location data collection and potential third-party data sharing practices.
AccuWeather has been associated with sharing precise location data with third-party monetisation firms even when users opt out of location sharing within the app.
Publisher has been associated with aggressive data collection practices and has had apps removed from the Google Play Store for policy violations related to ad fraud and data handling.
Free utility apps in this category have historically been associated with excessive permission requests and potential third-party data sharing practices beyond their stated function.

Free QR/barcode scanner apps have historically been associated with requesting location permissions beyond what is required for their core function, and with potential third-party data sharing practices.
Free flashlight utility apps have historically been flagged for requesting location and device permissions far beyond what is required for their stated function.

WiFi analyzer apps require location permissions to scan nearby networks on Android, but free ad-supported versions in this category have been associated with potential third-party data sharing practices.
App has been subject to removal from the Google Play Store for policy violations. Publisher has been associated with data collection practices beyond the app's stated function.
App requires access to contacts and device data to function. Publisher has been associated with collecting and aggregating user data including location information from its large user base.
Phone booster and cleaner apps have historically been associated with excessive permission requests and potential third-party data sharing practices beyond their stated function.
Free VPN apps have historically been associated with data logging and selling user data — including location information — to third parties, which is counter to their stated privacy purpose.
Free battery saver apps have historically been associated with requesting permissions beyond their stated function and potential third-party data sharing practices.
Free-to-play casino and slots games have historically been associated with extensive data collection and potential third-party data sharing practices to support ad targeting.
Free-to-play casino and slots games have historically been associated with extensive data collection and potential third-party data sharing practices to support ad targeting.
Publisher has been subject to regulatory investigations related to child-directed apps and data collection practices.

Core Android middleware providing location APIs, authentication, maps, analytics, and cloud services to all apps. Location permission is system-fixed and cannot be revoked. Does not independently collect location — brokers access for other apps and Google services. Revoking mic/camera breaks Google Assistant and video calling. Privacy risk derives from trusting Google's API implementation and the individual apps that consume these APIs.
Background service that supports Meta's cross-app data collection and ad targeting infrastructure, including location data aggregation across Meta-affiliated apps.
Content recommendation and native advertising platform. Publisher operates a large-scale data collection network to support behavioural ad targeting, including location-based targeting.
Content discovery and native advertising platform. Publisher is primarily an ad network with associated data collection and targeting practices.
Mobile advertising and app monetisation platform. Core business model is built around data collection and behavioural targeting, including location data.

Dating apps collect precise location data as a core function. Apps in this category have historically been associated with sharing location and personal data with third-party advertisers and data brokers.

Dating apps collect precise location data as a core function. Apps in this category have historically been associated with sharing location and personal data with third-party advertisers and data brokers.

Dating apps collect precise location data as a core function. Apps in this category have historically been associated with sharing location and personal data with third-party advertisers and data brokers.
App has been subject to regulatory investigations and public reporting related to sharing precise location and sensitive personal data with third-party advertisers.

System setup wizard used only during initial device configuration. Location access is used for locale/region detection during setup and is not actively used post-setup. Revoking permissions may interfere with factory reset setup flow.

Provides on-device ML features including Now Playing, Live Caption, Smart Compose, and app predictions. Location access supports contextual suggestions. Processing occurs on-device. Revoking location may reduce context-aware prediction quality but will not break core device functionality.

Citizens Broadband Radio Service monitor required by FCC Part 96 rules for shared spectrum coordination. Location access is mandated by federal regulation for spectrum allocation and interference avoidance. Revoking location permission may disrupt CBRS-band connectivity on supported carriers. Tier reflects regulatory data transmission requirement.

System service that manages Bluetooth/Wi-Fi pairing with companion devices such as smartwatches and fitness trackers. Location access is required by Android for Bluetooth LE scanning. Revoking location may prevent pairing or communication with companion devices.

Core location provider that supplies fused location data to all apps via Google Play Services APIs. This is the system location engine, not a standalone data collector. Permissions are system-fixed and cannot be revoked by the user. Disabling would break location functionality for all apps on the device.

Google Search, Assistant, and Discover hub. Holds broad permissions including location, microphone, camera, phone, SMS, contacts, and call logs. Location powers local results and Assistant context. Microphone is used for voice search and Assistant. Phone/SMS/contacts/call log access supports caller ID, spam detection, and Assistant calling features. Permissions can be individually revoked in Settings > Apps with minimal impact — location removal limits local results, microphone removal disables voice features, phone/SMS/contacts removal disables caller ID and related Assistant features. Tier reflects breadth of data access.

Manages Google Timeline (formerly Location History), which records and stores device location data over time to Google servers. This is the most privacy-significant system location component — it persists historical location data beyond immediate use. Users can disable Timeline in Google Account settings. Revoking location permission limits new data collection but does not delete existing history. Tier 3 reflects persistent off-device location data storage.

IP Multimedia Subsystem service providing VoLTE calling and Wi-Fi calling. Location access supports E911 emergency location reporting as required by FCC regulations. Revoking location may impair emergency call location accuracy and could affect VoLTE/Wi-Fi calling reliability. Tier reflects regulatory location transmission for emergency services.

Extension for Quick Share (formerly Nearby Share) file transfer. Location access is required by Android for Wi-Fi Direct and Bluetooth device discovery. Revoking location will prevent Quick Share from discovering nearby devices. Data transfer is peer-to-peer and local.

Manages satellite-based emergency SOS communication on supported devices. Location access is essential for transmitting position data to emergency responders via satellite. Revoking location would defeat the purpose of satellite emergency communication. Only active during satellite SOS sessions.

Provides search indexing, suggestions, and contextual features within the Android Settings app. Location access supports location-related setting suggestions and contextual recommendations. Revoking location may reduce relevance of Settings suggestions but will not impair core Settings functionality. Tier reflects data processing for personalization within system settings.

Android Debug Bridge (ADB) shell process. All permissions are system-fixed and cannot be revoked by the user — this is normal Android architecture, not a security concern. Shell only executes commands when a computer is physically connected via USB with debugging enabled, or when explicitly authorized via wireless debugging. No background data collection occurs. Disabling developer options removes ADB access entirely.

System service related to media playback and content rendering. Location access, if present, supports location-based content or service availability features. This is a background system component with no standalone user interface. Revoking permissions may affect media playback or related system functionality.

publisher_overrides.basis: Publisher has historically been associated with large-scale data monetisation and third-party data sharing practices across its app portfolio.
Publisher has been subject to regulatory scrutiny in multiple jurisdictions related to data collection and potential third-party data sharing practices.
Publisher operates primarily as an ad network and has been associated with broad data collection practices across integrated apps.
Publisher operates as an ad monetisation platform and has been associated with extensive device and behavioural data collection practices.
Publisher has a well-documented commitment to end-to-end encryption and minimal data collection. No known third-party data sharing practices.
Publisher is a privacy-focused company with audited, open-source applications and a documented no-data-sharing policy.
Publisher is a non-profit with publicly documented privacy policies and no known data brokerage practices.
Publisher's core product and business model are built on user privacy. No known third-party data sharing or location data monetisation practices.
Open-source password manager with audited code and documented minimal data collection practices.

Core Android OS component. Permissions are structurally required for system functionality and are typically non-revocable or essential for device operation (e.g., emergency services, telephony, debugging). Not a standalone data collector.

Google Play Services system framework and sub-components. Permissions support core device services including location APIs, connectivity, and system integration. Revoking permissions may impair device functionality for all dependent apps.
FCC-mandated CBRS spectrum coordination service. Location access is required by federal regulation (Part 96) for shared spectrum allocation. Not a user-facing data collector.

Samsung Shannon baseband modem component. Permissions support telephony functions including VoLTE, Wi-Fi calling, and E911 emergency location reporting. Revoking permissions may impair calling and emergency services.
Satellite emergency SOS gateway service. Location access is essential for transmitting position data to emergency responders via satellite. Only active during satellite SOS sessions.

metadata.description: Privacy Auditor seed risk database. Category risk tiers: 1=Low, 2=Medium, 3=High. Combined with permission aggressiveness score to produce final risk indicator.
metadata.scoring_note: Risk indicators are based on app category, permission configuration, and publicly available information. They are provided for general awareness only and do not constitute definitive claims about any app or developer's data practices.
